title: Intravascular ultrasound catheter
abstract: Intravascular imaging catheters and methods for making and using intravascular imaging catheters are disclosed. An example intravascular imaging catheter may include an elongate catheter shaft having a distal end region and a proximal region. The distal end region may include a side port. An imaging core may be disposed within the elongate catheter shaft. The imaging core may include an imaging device configured to be slidable within the elongate catheter shaft and through the side port.
claims: Claims Claims What is claimed is: 1. An intravascular imaging catheter, comprising: an elongate catheter shaft having a distal end region and a proximal region; wherein the distal end region includes a side port; an imaging core disposed within the elongate catheter shaft; and wherein the imaging core includes an imaging device configured to be slidable within the elongate catheter shaft and through the side port. 2. The intravascular imaging catheter of claim 1, wherein the distal end region includes a tip having a guidewire lumen formed therein. 3. The intravascular imaging catheter of any one of claims 1-2, wherein the distal end region includes a tip and wherein the side port is formed in the tip. 4. The intravascular imaging catheter of any one of claims 1-3, wherein the imaging device includes an ultrasound transducer. 5. The intravascular imaging catheter of any one of claims 1-4, wherein the imaging device is configured to image at an angle that is substantially normal to the imaging core. 6. The intravascular imaging catheter of any one of claims 1-5, wherein the imaging device is configured to image in a distal direction at an angle less than about 90 degrees relative to the imaging core. 7. The intravascular imaging catheter of any one of claims 1-6, wherein the imaging device includes one or more distally-angled transducers. 8. The intravascular imaging catheter of any one of claims 1-7, wherein the imaging device is configured to shift between a first orientation and a second orientation. 9. The intravascular imaging catheter of claim 8, wherein the imaging device is configured to image at an angle that is substantially normal to the imaging core when the imaging device is in the first orientation. 10. The intravascular imaging catheter of any one of claims 8-9, wherein the imaging device is configured to image in a distal direction at an angle less than about 90 degrees relative to the imaging core when the imaging device is in the second orientation. 11. The intravascular imaging catheter of any one of claims 1-10, wherein the imaging device is configured to image in a distal direction at a variable angle. 12. The intravascular imaging catheter of any one of claims 1-11, wherein the imaging device is mounted upon an inflatable member. 13. The intravascular imaging catheter of claim 12, wherein the imaging device includes a plurality of transducers. 14. The intravascular imaging catheter of any one of claims 12-13, wherein the inflatable member has a guidewire lumen formed therein. 15. The intravascular imaging catheter of any one of claims 1-14, wherein the elongate catheter shaft includes a torque-transmitting reinforcing member.
